name: cross-margin-view
description: Self-contained walkthrough of cross-asset margin. The scenario builds the borrower's state from scratch: register + verify, deposit two collateral mints, open a cross-mint loan, then read the unified margin view. Useful for asking: how does the pool change as I tighten the EQUITY haircut? What happens to free margin when the cash mint's Pyth price moves? Where does cross-mint netting change the verdict vs siloed lending health?
headline: Build one borrower with two collateral mints + one cross-mint loan, then watch free margin recompute in one read-only call.

steps.explanation: Initialize the on-chain compliance registry (idempotent for a fresh validator).
Register the borrower in the compliance registry.
Verify the borrower so they can participate in DvP / vault / lending flows.
Initialize the RiskParams PDA. Sets the governance-mutable haircut table that the margin view reads on every call.
(Off-CLI) Create two Token-2022 mints (collateral + cash) without TransferHook and mint inventory to the borrower. Capture the resulting public keys as MINT_SOVEREIGN and MINT_USDC.
Initialize the collateral vault for the sovereign-class mint.
Initialize the cash vault for the loan's settlement currency.
Borrower deposits 500_000 units of collateral into the sovereign vault.
(Lender side) The lender deposits cash inventory so a loan can be opened against it.
Open a cross-mint loan: borrower collateralizes 300_000 sovereign units, borrows 200_000 USDC against it.

Read the unified margin view across both collateral mints — this is the punchline. Haircut-adjusted collateral pool, projected liability (principal + accrued simple interest), and net free margin per cash mint, all in one read-only call.
(Parameter dial) Tighten the sovereign-bond haircut and re-read the margin view to see the pool drop.
Re-read margin. Same accounts, same loan; the haircut change alone moves the free-margin number.
